title: Ctenocladium mildbraedii
uses: Ctenocladium mildbraedii has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, particularly its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is said to have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. taxonomy: Ctenocladium mildbraedii belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, which is a family of ferns. This species is classified as a genus within the family. The genus Ctenocladium is further classified as a part of the subfamily Grammitidoideae. This fern is native to tropical regions and is commonly found in shaded areas. The classification of Ctenocladium mildbraedii is still being studied and refined by botanists.

preparations: Ctenocladium mildbraedii, a plant native to tropical Africa, has been traditionally used in herbal preparations for its medicinal properties.
